questions.q: Which ion is primarily responsible for the depolarization phase of a neuronal action potential?
Which ion is primarily responsible for the repolarization phase of a neuronal action potential?
Which hormone is synthesized in the hypothalamus and stored in the posterior pituitary to regulate water retention?
Which hormone is responsible for milk ejection and uterine contractions?
Which gastric cells secrete Hydrochloric Acid (HCl) and Intrinsic Factor?
Which gastric cells secrete Pepsinogen?
What phenomenon describes the rightward shift of the O2-hemoglobin dissociation curve due to increased PCO2 and decreased pH?
What phenomenon describes how deoxygenated blood can carry more CO2 than oxygenated blood?
Which ion influx triggers the exocytosis of neurotransmitter vesicles at the axon terminal?
In skeletal muscle, calcium binds to which protein to initiate contraction?
In smooth muscle, calcium binds to which protein to initiate contraction?
What is the normal natural pacemaker of the heart?
Phase 0 (rapid depolarization) of the ventricular muscle action potential is mediated by which ion?
Phase 2 (plateau) of the ventricular muscle action potential is mediated by which ion?
Phase 0 of the SA node action potential is mediated by which ion?
In which segment of the nephron is 65% of Na+ and H2O, and 100% of glucose reabsorbed?
Which hormone is produced by the kidneys in response to hypoxia to stimulate RBC production?
Which pancreatic cells are responsible for secreting Insulin?
Which pancreatic cells are responsible for secreting Glucagon?
Which hormone lowers blood calcium levels by inhibiting osteoclast activity?
What is the primary excitatory neurotransmitter in the Central Nervous System?
What is the primary inhibitory neurotransmitter in the Brain?
What is the primary inhibitory neurotransmitter in the Spinal Cord?
What is the primary muscle of normal quiet inspiration?
The volume of air inspired or expired with each normal breath is called?
The maximum volume of air that can be exhaled after a maximal inspiration is called?
Which enzyme converts Angiotensin I to the active vasoconstrictor Angiotensin II?
From which specific cells in the kidney is Renin secreted?
Which vitamin is required for the active intestinal absorption of Calcium?
Which GI hormone stimulates gallbladder contraction and pancreatic enzyme secretion?
Which GI hormone stimulates pancreatic bicarbonate secretion to neutralize stomach acid?
Which receptor subtype is responsible for parasympathetic slowing of the heart rate?
Which receptor subtype mediates sympathetically induced bronchodilation?
Which substance is the gold standard for measuring Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR) because it is freely filtered but neither reabsorbed nor secreted?
Which substance is used to estimate Effective Renal Plasma Flow (ERPF)?
Which law states that stroke volume of the heart increases in response to an increase in end-diastolic volume?
An increase in central venous pressure causing an increase in heart rate describes which reflex?
What unique physiological response do pulmonary arterioles have to local alveolar hypoxia?
questions.a: Hypoxic Pulmonary Vasoconstriction (to divert blood to well-ventilated areas)

questions.q: What is the term for a condition where the V/Q ratio equals infinity (ventilation with zero perfusion)?
What is the term for a condition where the V/Q ratio equals zero (perfusion with zero ventilation)?
A mutation in which ion channel is responsible for the pathophysiology of Cystic Fibrosis?
Which receptor type mediates sympathetically induced pupil dilation (mydriasis)?
Which receptor type mediates sympathetic Renin release from the kidneys?
Which hormones increase basal metabolic rate primarily by increasing Na+/K+ ATPase activity?
What are the two primary physiological stimuli for Aldosterone secretion?
Which enzyme deficiency is the most common cause of Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (virilization and salt-wasting)?
What is the primary site of Magnesium (Mg2+) reabsorption in the nephron?
Which specific transporter is inhibited by loop diuretics like Furosemide?
Which specific transporter is inhibited by thiazide diuretics?
Which water channels are inserted into the apical membrane of the collecting duct under the influence of ADH?
Which sodium channels are upregulated in the collecting duct principal cells by Aldosterone?
According to Poiseuille's law, resistance to blood flow is inversely proportional to what factor raised to the 4th power?
What is the common intracellular second messenger for Glucagon and Beta-adrenergic receptors?
What is the common intracellular second messenger system for Alpha-1 receptors?
Which neurotransmitter is released by ALL preganglionic autonomic neurons (both sympathetic and parasympathetic)?
Postganglionic sympathetic innervation to eccrine sweat glands uses which unexpected neurotransmitter?
Which brain area is responsible for speech production, and damage causes expressive aphasia?
Which brain area is responsible for speech comprehension, and damage causes receptive aphasia?
What is the name of the white matter tract connecting Broca's and Wernicke's areas (damage causes conduction aphasia)?
The endolymph in the inner ear is unusual for an extracellular fluid because it has a high concentration of which ion?
A triad of bradycardia, hypotension, and apnea in response to noxious ventricular stimuli describes which reflex?
Autoregulation of cerebral blood flow is primarily driven by changes in which blood gas parameter?
During the cardiac cycle, what is the period where ventricular volume remains constant while ventricular pressure falls rapidly?
On a Jugular Venous Pulse (JVP) tracing, what does the 'a' wave represent?
On a JVP tracing, what does the 'v' wave represent?
questions.a: Venous filling of the right atrium against a closed tricuspid valve

questions.q: Which phase of the Valsalva maneuver features a drop in blood pressure and compensatory tachycardia due to decreased venous return?
The 'Treppe' or Bowditch effect (heart rate-induced increase in contractility) is primarily due to intracellular accumulation of what?
A reflex decrease in heart rate and peripheral vasoconstriction upon facial immersion in cold water is known as?

A prominent, rapid 'y' descent on a JVP tracing (Friedreich's sign) is classically seen in which cardiac condition?
Which physical principle explains why ventricular wall tension increases with ventricular dilation for a given pressure (T = P x r)?
What is the physiological mechanism by which the kidney maintains a hyperosmolar medullary interstitium?
Urea recycling in the inner medullary collecting duct is directly stimulated by which hormone?

A genetic defect in the Na+/Cl- cotransporter mimicking the chronic use of thiazide diuretics causes which syndrome?
A gain-of-function mutation in ENaC causing severe hypertension and hypokalemia (mimicking hyperaldosteronism but with low aldosterone) is?
Chemoreceptors in the aortic body transmit their signals to the medulla via which cranial nerve?
Chemoreceptors in the carotid body transmit their signals to the medulla via which cranial nerve?
What is the normal A-a (Alveolar-arterial) oxygen gradient value in a healthy young adult on room air?
The physiological shift of the O2-hemoglobin dissociation curve to the right at high altitudes is mediated by increased RBC synthesis of?
In endocrine physiology, TRH stimulates prolactin release. What physiological substance acts as the primary inhibitor of prolactin?
The temporary physiological inhibition of thyroid hormone synthesis in response to an acutely high iodine load is called?
Thyrotoxicosis developing after an iodine load in a patient with a pre-existing iodine-deficiency goiter is known as?
Which hypothalamic hormone directly inhibits the anterior pituitary secretion of both Growth Hormone and TSH?
Gastric slow waves (basal electrical rhythm) are generated by a network of pacemaker cells called?
Which gastrointestinal hormone inhibits gastric acid secretion and stimulates insulin release (the Incretin effect)?
What is the physiological effect of VIP (Vasoactive Intestinal Peptide) on the Lower Esophageal Sphincter (LES)?
In the basal ganglia direct pathway, movement is facilitated by the ultimate disinhibition of which structure?
In the basal ganglia indirect pathway, movement is inhibited by exciting which structure to increase its inhibitory output?
questions.a: Globus Pallidus internus (GPi) / Substantia Nigra pars reticulata

questions.q: Physiological damage to the Subthalamic Nucleus (STN) results in which violent movement disorder?
On an EEG, Sleep Spindles and K-complexes are the physiological hallmark of which sleep stage?
Night terrors and sleepwalking (somnambulism) typically occur during which physiological stage of sleep?
Which interneurons in the spinal cord provide recurrent collateral inhibition to alpha motor neurons?
What specific neurotransmitter is utilized by Renshaw cells to mediate recurrent spinal inhibition?
Which visual pathway is physiologically responsible for transmitting high-resolution central vision, form, and color?
Which visual pathway is physiologically responsible for transmitting motion and spatial analysis?
What specific sensor in the muscle detects changes in muscle length and the rate of change in length?
What specific sensor in the tendon detects changes in muscle tension to prevent avulsion?
